overview: A local-first web app that lets Royal Caribbean content authors search, browse, and upload digital assets from the AEM DAM export. A React frontend communicates with an Express backend backed by SQLite. Assets are ingested from existing .content.xml metadata files, enriched with AI vision (OpenAI GPT-4o) on upload, and made searchable via natural language full-text search and structured filters.
goals: Ingest all 49 existing DAM assets (XML metadata + thumbnails) into SQLite on startup
Provide natural language full-text search across all metadata fields
Support structured filters: category, subcategory, rights status, location, metadata quality flags
Show asset detail popup with original metadata vs AI-enriched metadata side by side
Allow upload of single images or folders — auto-enrich with OpenAI Vision on upload
Highlight metadata quality issues (missing fields, rights problems, sync errors, release placeholders)
nonGoals: No user authentication or session management
No editing or updating of existing .content.xml files
No Celebrity Cruises assets in scope
No bulk export or download features
successMetrics: All 49 existing assets appear in the browse grid on first load
Natural language query returns relevant results within 200ms
Uploaded image is enriched and searchable within 10 seconds
Asset detail modal shows original and enriched fields side by side
All quality gates pass: npm test, npm run lint, npm run build
openQuestions: Should uploaded assets be stored inside Data/royal/ to match existing structure, or in a separate uploads/ directory?
Should the AI enrichment call happen synchronously on upload (blocking response) or asynchronously (polling)?
What OpenAI model version should be used — gpt-4o or gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency?

stack.ai: OpenAI GPT-4o Vision API (OPENAI_API_KEY env var)

layout: app/client/src/api/         - Fetch wrapper for Express API

routes.name: React App Shell
routes.purpose: Serve the React SPA (Vite dev server in development)

routes.name: Search Assets
routes.purpose: Full-text + structured filter search. Query params: q, category, subcategory, rights_status, location, has_title, has_rights, has_location, limit, offset

routes.name: Asset Detail
routes.purpose: Return single asset with all original and enriched metadata fields

routes.name: Filter Options
routes.purpose: Return all distinct filter values: categories, subcategories, locations, rights statuses

routes.name: Upload Asset(s)
routes.purpose: Accept multipart/form-data with one or more image files. Save to uploads/, insert to DB, trigger AI enrichment, return enriched asset records

routes.name: Re-enrich Asset
routes.purpose: Trigger AI enrichment for an existing asset by ID. Useful for re-processing.

uiNotes: Brand colors: Navy #001B6B (primary), Gold #C8960C (accent), white backgrounds
Header: Royal Caribbean wordmark + app title + upload button
Main layout: left filter sidebar (260px) + right asset grid (fluid)
Asset grid: responsive CSS grid, min 200px cards, 16:9 thumbnail aspect ratio
Asset card: thumbnail, title (or filename fallback), category badge, rights status indicator (green/amber/red dot)
Asset detail: modal/drawer overlay, left side = large image, right side = two-column metadata (Original | Enriched)
Quality issue badges: missing title, missing rights, S7 sync error, release placeholder — shown as small icons on card and detailed in modal
Upload component: drag-and-drop zone + file picker button, supports multiple files and folder selection (webkitdirectory), shows progress per file
Search bar: prominent at top, natural language placeholder text, debounced 300ms
Filter sidebar sections: Category, Subcategory, Rights Status, Location, Metadata Quality (toggle chips)
Active filters shown as removable chips below search bar
Empty state: friendly message when no results with suggested filters to clear

dataModel.description: Main table storing all asset metadata — both original from XML and AI-enriched
SQLite FTS5 virtual table for natural language full-text search

rules: Display title: use enriched_title if present, else original_title, else filename (without extension)
Display description: use enriched_description if present, else original_description
Display tags: merge enriched_tags and original_tags, deduplicate
Rights status: 'owned' if rights_owner contains 'Royal Caribbean' (case-insensitive, trimmed); 'unclear' if rights data present but has typo or inconsistency; 'none' if no rights data
Channel inference (deterministic): if filename contains 'hero' → hero; 'banner' → banner; 'mobile' or height > width → mobile/vertical; 'desktop' → desktop
Dimensions → format: width > height → landscape; height > width → portrait; width == height → square
Creator normalization: '[Royal Carribean]', 'Brand Team', 'Brand Team ', '[Brand Team]', '[Production Team ]' → 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'
Release placeholder detection: if any field value contains 'Selecione' → set has_release_placeholder = 1
FTS sync: after any insert or update to assets table, sync the corresponding FTS row
Uploaded files are stored in app/uploads/<uuid>-<originalname>
On server startup, run XML ingest if assets table is empty

stories.title: Scaffold project structure
stories.description: As a developer, I want a working monorepo with Express backend and React Vite frontend so that I can build and test both apps from a single root.
acceptanceCriteria: Root app/ directory contains package.json with workspaces: ['client', 'server']
app/server/ is an Express app: npm install express better-sqlite3 multer cors dotenv openai
app/server/ dev script uses nodemon, test script uses jest
app/client/ is a React + Vite app: npm create vite@latest with react template
app/client/ installs: npm install axios
app/client/ test script uses vitest and @testing-library/react
Root package.json has scripts: dev (runs both client and server concurrently), test (runs both test suites), lint, build
npm install concurrently at root for parallel dev
app/server/src/index.js starts Express on port 3001 with a GET /api/health → { status: 'ok' }

app/client/src/App.jsx renders

Royal Caribbean DAM
.env.example at root with OPENAI_API_KEY=, PORT=3001, DATA_DIR=../Data/royal
.gitignore includes: node_modules, dam.sqlite, uploads/, .env
Example: running npm run dev from app/ starts both servers without error
Negative case: missing OPENAI_API_KEY in .env does not crash startup — server logs a warning and disables AI enrichment

stories.title: Database schema and migrations
stories.description: As a developer, I want SQLite tables created automatically on startup so that the app has persistent storage without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: app/server/src/db/schema.js exports a runMigrations(db) function
runMigrations creates the assets table with all fields defined in the data model
runMigrations creates the assets_fts FTS5 virtual table with content='assets'
runMigrations is idempotent — running it twice does not error or duplicate tables (use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
app/server/src/db/index.js opens dam.sqlite, calls runMigrations, and exports the db instance
Jest test: runMigrations on an in-memory SQLite DB creates both tables successfully
Jest test: inserting a row into assets and querying assets_fts returns the same row
Negative case: if dam.sqlite directory is not writable, server logs a clear error and exits with code 1

stories.title: XML ingest service — parse DAM export into SQLite
stories.description: As a developer, I want all existing .content.xml asset files parsed and loaded into SQLite on startup so that the 49 DAM assets are immediately available.
acceptanceCriteria: app/server/src/services/ingest.js exports an ingestAssets(db, dataDir) function
Function recursively walks dataDir, finds all .content.xml files where the parent directory name ends in .jpg, .jpeg, or .png and the path does not contain _jcr_content

For each XML file, parses dc:title, dc:description, dc:creator, dc:rights, xmpRights:Owner, xmpRights:UsageTerms, Iptc4xmpExt:LocationShown, tiff:ImageWidth, tiff:ImageLength, dam:size, dam:Fileformat, dam:MIMEtype, dam:scene7File, dam:scene7Domain, dam:scene7FileStatus, dam:lastS7SyncStatus, cq:parentPath, cq:name, cq:tags
Strips JCR type wrappers: {Long}1920 → 1920, {Boolean}true → true, {Date}2021-... → string
Infers category, subcategory, subsubcategory from folder path segments
Applies deterministic enrichment: creator normalization, rights reconciliation, channel/format inference from filename and dimensions, release placeholder detection
Sets thumbnail_path and web_image_path relative to DATA_DIR if those rendition files exist on disk
Inserts using INSERT OR IGNORE so re-running ingest does not duplicate rows
On server startup (server/src/index.js), calls ingestAssets if SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assets returns 0
Jest test: ingestAssets on the real Data/royal directory inserts exactly 49 rows into assets
Jest test: asset 'anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg' has original_title 'Anthem of the Seas Sightseeing the Statue of Liberty, New York'
Jest test: creator '[Royal Carribean]' is normalized to 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'
Jest test: 7 assets have has_release_placeholder = 1
Jest test: 6 assets have s7_sync_error = 1
Negative case: malformed XML file is skipped with a console.warn, not a thrown error

stories.title: GET /api/assets — search and filter endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and filter assets via the API so that I can find relevant images for my campaign.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets returns { total, assets: [...] } with all 49 assets when no query params
Query param q performs FTS5 full-text search across title, description, tags, location, creator, category, subcategory, filename
Query param category filters by exact category value (ships or promotions)
Query param subcategory filters by exact subcategory (allure, anthem, grandeur, asia, casino, star-and-icon)
Query param rights_status filters by: owned, unclear, none
Query param location filters by exact LocationShown value
Query param has_title=true/false filters assets with/without a title
Query param has_rights=true/false filters assets with/without any rights data
Query param has_release_placeholder=true filters the 7 flagged assets
Query params limit (default 100, max 200) and offset (default 0) for pagination

Response asset objects include: id, filename, category, subcategory, display_title, display_description, display_tags, thumbnail_path, web_image_path, rights_status, enrichment_source, width, height, scene7_file, scene7_domain, s7_sync_error, needs_rights_review, has_release_placeholder
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?q=sunset returns assets containing 'sunset' in any text field
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?category=ships returns only ship assets
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?rights_status=none returns exactly 27 assets
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?limit=5&offset=0 returns 5 assets with total=49
Negative case: GET /api/assets?q=zzznomatch returns { total: 0, assets: [] } with 200 status

stories.title: GET /api/assets/:id — asset detail endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to retrieve full metadata for a single asset so that I can review all original and enriched fields.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets/:id returns the full asset record with all fields including original_* and enriched_* columns
Response includes a cdn_url field constructed as scene7_domain + 'is/image/' + scene7_file

Response includes a quality_issues array listing any problems: 'missing_title', 'missing_description', 'missing_rights', 'missing_location', 'title_equals_description', 's7_sync_error', 'release_placeholder'
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg returns original_title correctly
Supertest test: returned quality_issues for an asset with no rights data includes 'missing_rights'
Negative case: GET /api/assets/nonexistent-id returns 404 { error: 'Asset not found' }

stories.title: GET /api/filters — available filter values endpoint
stories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a filters endpoint so that the UI can dynamically populate filter options without hardcoding.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/filters returns { categories, subcategories, locations, rights_statuses, counts }
categories is a sorted array of distinct category values
subcategories is an object keyed by category with arrays of subcategory values
locations is a sorted array of distinct non-null LocationShown values
rights_statuses is ['owned', 'unclear', 'none'] with counts for each
counts object includes: total, missing_title, missing_rights, has_release_placeholder, s7_sync_error
Supertest test: categories contains 'ships' and 'promotions'
Supertest test: subcategories.ships contains 'allure', 'anthem', 'grandeur'
Negative case: if database is empty, returns empty arrays with all counts as 0

stories.title: POST /api/assets/upload — image upload endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to upload image files to the DAM so that new assets are stored and made searchable.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install multer at server level
POST /api/assets/upload accepts multipart/form-data with field name 'files' (supports multiple files)
Each uploaded file is saved to app/uploads/<uuid>-<sanitized-originalname>
For each file, a new asset row is inserted into assets with: id=uuid, filename=originalname, filepath=uploads path, category='uploaded', enrichment_source='pending'
Response is { uploaded: N, assets: [{ id, filename, filepath }] } with 201 status
Supported MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp — reject others with 400
Max file size per upload: 20MB — reject with 413 if exceeded
Supertest test: uploading a valid JPEG returns 201 with asset id
Supertest test: uploaded asset is retrievable via GET /api/assets/:id
Supertest test: uploaded file exists on disk at the returned filepath
Negative case: uploading a .txt file returns 400 { error: 'Unsupported file type' }
Negative case: uploading a file >20MB returns 413

stories.title: POST /api/assets/:id/enrich — AI vision enrichment endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want uploaded images to be enriched with AI-generated metadata so that new assets are immediately searchable and described.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/assets/:id/enrich reads the asset's filepath, sends the image to OpenAI GPT-4o Vision as base64
Prompt instructs model to return JSON with: title, description, tags (array), location (or null), scene, subjects (array), mood, channel_hint, confidence (0-1)

On success, updates assets table: enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags (JSON string), enriched_location, enriched_scene, enriched_subjects (JSON string), enriched_mood, enriched_channel, enrichment_source='ai-vision', enrichment_confidence
FTS table is updated after enrichment so new asset appears in natural language search
Response is the updated full asset record with 200 status
If OPENAI_API_KEY is not set, returns 503 { error: 'AI enrichment unavailable — OPENAI_API_KEY not configured' }
Jest test: enrichment service called with a mock OpenAI client correctly maps response fields to DB columns
Jest test: after enrichment, GET /api/assets/:id returns non-null enriched_title
Negative case: if OpenAI API returns an error, returns 502 { error: 'Enrichment failed', detail: '...' } — asset row is not corrupted
Negative case: if asset has no filepath (XML-ingested asset without local image), returns 400 { error: 'No image file available for enrichment' }

stories.title: React app shell, layout, and API client
stories.description: As a developer, I want a React app shell with a branded header, layout, and typed API client so that all pages share a consistent structure.
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx renders a top-level layout with: <Header />, <main> content area
Header component shows 'Royal Caribbean' wordmark in navy, 'DAM Asset Intelligence' subtitle in gold, and an Upload button on the right
Layout uses CSS: navy header (#001B6B), gold accent bar, white/light-grey main area
app/client/src/api/assets.js exports: getAssets(params), getAsset(id), getFilters(), uploadFiles(formData), enrichAsset(id)
All API functions use axios with base URL http://localhost:3001
Vitest test: Header renders the text 'Royal Caribbean'
Vitest test: Header renders an 'Upload' button
Negative case: if API base URL is unreachable, axios errors are caught and surfaced — app does not crash with an unhandled rejection

stories.title: Search and browse page
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and browse all assets on the main page so that I can find the right image for my campaign.
acceptanceCriteria: Route / renders the search/browse page
SearchBar component at top: text input with placeholder 'Search assets...', debounced 300ms, calls getAssets({ q }) on change

FilterSidebar (260px left panel) with collapsible sections: Category (ships/promotions chips), Subcategory (dynamic from filters API), Rights Status (owned/unclear/none chips), Location (dropdown), Metadata Quality (toggles for missing title, missing rights, release placeholder)
Active filters shown as removable chips below the search bar
AssetGrid renders assets in a responsive CSS grid (min-width 200px columns)
AssetCard shows: thumbnail (with fallback placeholder if no thumbnail), display_title, category badge, rights status dot (green=owned, amber=unclear, red=none), quality warning icon if any issues
Results count shown above grid: 'Showing X of Y assets'
Loading skeleton cards shown while API request is in-flight
Empty state message when results are 0: 'No assets match your search. Try clearing some filters.'
Clicking an asset card opens the AssetDetailModal (US-011)
Vitest test: renders 49 asset cards when API returns all assets
Vitest test: typing in search bar triggers getAssets with the q param after debounce
Vitest test: selecting category 'ships' calls getAssets with category='ships'
Negative case: API error shows an error banner, not a blank screen

stories.title: Asset detail modal
stories.description: As a content author, I want to open an asset detail popup so that I can review all metadata, compare original vs enriched fields, and copy the CDN URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an AssetCard opens AssetDetailModal as a full-screen overlay with close button (X) and ESC key support
Modal left panel: large image (web_image_path at 1280px if available, else thumbnail), filename, dimensions (WxH px), file size, format
Modal right panel: two-column metadata table with headers 'Original' and 'Enriched'
Metadata rows: Title, Description, Tags, Location, Creator, Rights Owner, Usage Terms, Channel, Format
Enriched column shows AI-generated value in navy text; empty cells show a grey dash
If enriched value differs from original, enriched cell has a subtle gold left border
Quality Issues section below metadata table: lists each issue as a badge (e.g. 'Missing Rights', 'Release Placeholder — needs review')
CDN URL section: full URL displayed in a monospace code block with a 'Copy' button; copy triggers navigator.clipboard.writeText and shows 'Copied!' for 2 seconds
If asset has no enriched data, shows 'Not yet enriched' banner with an 'Enrich with AI' button that calls enrichAsset(id) and refreshes the modal
Vitest test: modal renders asset title from display_title
Vitest test: clicking Copy button updates button text to 'Copied!'
Vitest test: pressing ESC closes the modal
Negative case: if cdn_url is null (no scene7_file), CDN section is hidden

stories.title: Upload component
stories.description: As a content author, I want to upload new images via a drag-and-drop interface so that they are stored and auto-enriched with AI metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Upload' in the Header opens UploadModal as an overlay
UploadModal contains a drag-and-drop zone with text 'Drop images here or click to browse'
File input accepts image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp and supports multiple selection (multiple attribute)
Folder upload supported via <input webkitdirectory> toggle button labeled 'Upload folder'
After file selection, shows a file list with: filename, file size, status (pending/uploading/uploaded/enriching/done/error)
On submit, calls uploadFiles(formData) — each file shows 'uploading' status
On upload success, immediately calls enrichAsset(id) for each uploaded asset — status changes to 'enriching'
On enrichment complete, status changes to 'done' with a green checkmark
After all files complete, shows 'View assets' button that closes modal and refreshes the asset grid
Vitest test: dropping a JPEG file onto the drop zone adds it to the file list
Vitest test: submit button is disabled when file list is empty
Vitest test: each file shows 'done' status after mock upload + enrich calls succeed
Negative case: if a file exceeds 20MB, it is rejected client-side before upload with 'File too large (max 20MB)'
Negative case: if enrichment fails (503 — no API key), file shows 'Uploaded, enrichment unavailable' status — asset is still stored
